summary: Muddy matrix recording of a strong 1987 Chicago run featuring a standout Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition and a soaring China Doll.
review: The show is widely praised as one of the best of the three‑night ‘El’ run, with a tight first set and an epic second‑set suite that highlights the classic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, Playing in the Band and a glowing China Doll.  The source is a matrix mix that suffers from muddiness and lacks the clarity of a clean soundboard, but the performance energy more than makes up for the technical shortcomings.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating from reviewers is ~4.2 and most comments are enthusiastic about the music; recording quality is noted as muddy, pulling the overall score down slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the sound as very muddy with no cleaner soundboard release available; overall listenable but lacks clarity and detail.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: A concise first set followed by a massive second‑set suite (PITB→UJB→d/s→China Doll→Playing Jam→Greatest Rockin' Texas Fun Bowl→Sugar Magnolia) that keeps the momentum high, though some reviewers note the ending feels a bit rushed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as healthy and back in form; strong vocals on China Doll and solid guitar tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Brent) is noted as fired up and energetic, though his song "Never Trust a Woman" lacked the excitement of earlier versions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) praised for getting more comfortable and delivering strong parts on Good Time Blues and Far from Me.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is highlighted in a ~15‑minute drums/space segment; overall energy noted as youthful.
